title: Dopamine Is Not the Pleasure Molecule
script_summary: Dopamine drives motivation and reward prediction, not just pleasure.

segments.voiceover: Dopamine isn't happiness in a bottle.
It's your brain's signal for prediction and pursuit.
Dopamine spikes when we anticipate rewards, not when we consume them.
Neuroscience shows that it's about motivation. A reward prediction error means you're driven to chase goals.
So, next time you feel that rush, remember — it's not just pleasure. It's your brain gearing up for what's next.
Curious to learn more about how your brain works? Hit that follow button!
